text:
Under the banner of twilight, young Jamie strolled along the cobblestone lane in Savannah. The city whispered with history and salt air. He had stolen a loaf of crusty country bread and an old sketchbook from Aunt Edith's attic. Today's topic: ghost stories; he just needed inspiration.

Jamie spotted an antique bookstore glinting against the historic backdrop. "The Ghostly Gibbon," it called out. The sign fluttered ominously in the gentle breeze. Eager to delve into some creepy tales, he pushed through its creaky doors.

Inside, an elderly lady with a lace apron perched on her hips beckoned him behind the counter.

"Good evening!" she said. "I've got a shelf full of frights. Pick anything at random."

Jamie flipped through pages until his finger landed on a sepia-toned illustration of an apelike creature. "The Ghostly Gibbon," it read. A chill crept up his spine.
